channel.title: Blogs on Lethrell's Profile
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Lethrell's Profile

item.title: Unethical Use Of AI Recognition
item.description:
Face Value: When Shopping Malls Secretly Read Our Faces — Ethics, Governance, and Soft Ethics in Practice

Introduction

Cadillac Fairview’s covert use of facial recognition inside mall directory kiosks is a vivid example of how biometric surveillance, weak consent practices, and opaque vendor relationships can collide with basic privacy expectations. This blog post examines that case through the lens of AI governance, ethical AI frameworks, and soft ethics, and draws practical lessons for data scientists, designers, and policy makers. It argues that technical safeguards alone are insufficient: meaningful consent, transparency, accountability, and purpose limitation must be embedded into both design and organizational practice.

item.title: Is Anonymity Real?
item.description:
This expansion explains why contradiction matters in practice, details the ethical and operational risks, and gives concrete, prioritized steps leaders can take to restore trust and protect respondents. The goal is to move from a technically misleading promise to a defensible, transparent practice that preserves respondent safety and data integrity.

The Illusion of Anonymity

“After a course, participants are given a survey of 10 questions. To complete the survey, participants open a URL in their web browser, available for a short period, and log in with their organization username and password. The survey prompts for the course name, but not their name. Instead, the survey description on this page indicates that all answers and the survey itself will remain anonymous.”
